blocks.content: Rian took the Signa and Exud and examined them.
It hurt his swordsman's pride to rely on someone else's weapon,
but he didn't want to argue about something like that while his friend was being held captive.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Thank you. But I might handle them roughly.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: It's okay. No, it doesn't matter if it breaks. Please, just save unnies.
Peope fell into thought.
Just a little while ago, the divine subjects were worried that their life expectancy was decreasing day by day.
But what about now? Each person took risks to destroy the misfortune of others.
Why does the law control humans?
To maintain population, control lifespans, and prevent them from leaving Heaven.
What kind of threat does an increasing human population pose to Ra?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Are you really going?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Peope couldn't understand herself asking this.
After meeting Shirone, everything changed.
He was the chaos itself. As if a chunk of rock thrown into the surface waters of heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You know I have no choice.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I was grateful earlier. For avoiding the fight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Actually, I didn't want to fight with you either.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I think we might understand each other, including the fact that you lightened Canya and Lena's sentences.
Peope tried hard to ignore Shirone's words.
She didn't want to hear about understanding each other.
How could a fairy and a human possibly understand each other?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please take good care of divine subjects from now on.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I return safely, we can become closer friends.
Turning toward an uncertain future, Shirone moved away.
Peope's little heart pounded.
Shirone's figure, following the Nors as he walked away, appeared blurry, as if shrouded in fog.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Then will you save me too?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course.
Peope lowered her head.
Of course? If that's the case, Shirone would die.
He was walking towards death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if you go to the First District, you won't be able to cross the gates of Zebul.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I have a way to rescue the girls. So, I'll go with you.
Shirone's eyes widened.
She said there was indeed a way to save them.
At this point, he couldn't even imagine what the method would be like.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really? Can you really save them?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't guarantee.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But if you go alone, you will surely die, so I'll go with you.
Shirone looked at Peope with a face full of gratitude.
Peope, whose face turned red, turned her head away in anger.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Hmph! Don't get any strange ideas.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I just felt a sense of responsibility to see this incident through to the end.
Ikirin accepted the current situation calmly.
It was against the law for the divine subjects to help Shirone and for Peope to help humans.
Does this have to happen in the end? No, if this is also an unchangeable law....
Ikirin, who had made a decision, opened her mouth.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: Peope, come to me.
Peope's shoulders flinched.
Now that he thought about it, he had made decisions on his own without the chief's permission.
Contrary to expectations, Ikirin was not angry.
She instead closed her eyes and transferred her memories to Peope.
New information began to flood Peope's head.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: Peope, take my memories.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: They will help you navigate Zebul.
Memory transfer among fairies was common,
but when someone of high rank transferred memories to someone of lower rank, it carried special significance.
For example, it was like a letter of appointment to perform duties on their behalf.
A senior of the 72nd rank fairy.
Realizing the sincerity of his sky-high senior,
Peope lowered his head,
remembering how he had been full of complaints and dissatisfaction.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Chief. I'm sorry for disappointing you again.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: I have never been disappointed in you.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: You are a fairy born during Era of Turbulence in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: From now on, everything that happens will differ from the laws you know.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: So, you must see and judge everything with your own eyes.
Peope's eyes were filled with emotion.
For the first time since she was born,
she saw Ikirin smile.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: You have a headstrong nature,

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: but that also means you learn through your own experiences.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: Remember that the spiral moves straight, looking in all directions.

blocks.speaker: Ikirin
blocks.content: I believe in you, Peope.
Peope wiped her tears and nodded firmly.
The fact that she was recognized by Ikirin boosted her courage a hundredfold.
Shirone and the group left the square,
taking Peope with them.
The portal controller led them to a Nors building not far from the central square.
The interior was complex like a maze,
with magic circles drawn in each room and various spirits residing within.
When they opened the iron door installed in the corner and entered,
the spirit of light was floating alone in the center of a dark room.
The controller approached and gently grasped the spirit.
As the spirit stopped moving,
his arm began to glow white.

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: By using the power of the spirit,

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: we can reach the First District in one go.

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: But it takes time to gather the affection.

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: It will take about five minutes.
Having experienced it in Purgatory,
they had no doubts.
While waiting,
Shirone looked at Peope floating at eye level.
She wiggled her small butt and fluttered her transparent wings.
Shirone playfully poked the cute little butt with her index finger.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Hey! What are you doing?
Even though she was a tiny fairy smaller than a palm,
she was still a girl.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you for your help.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's do our best together.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thanks to you, we have hope.
Peope pouted her lips.
But as if she had read Shirone's true feelings,
she turns back to face the light.
The five minutes of waiting passed slowly.
This phenomenon was also the relativity of time.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Is that guy Tabu really dead?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No, more precisely, is Mara a being that can be killed?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Before he died, he said something about returning if humanity falls again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, how should I explain this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He can be destroyed,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: but it's not a permanent death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mara is a being made of concepts,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: so even if he dies,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: he will eventually come back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In Heaven, they call it 'resetting'.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How long does it take to reset?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not set in stone,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: but he won't come back right away.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So if we kill him,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: he stays dead for a while.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, that's right.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But if we look at it from another perspective...

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: That's enough.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: For now, that's all I need to know.
Canis thought of nothing but battle.
To get Arin back,
he must become stronger than he is now.
He started simulating his mental strength improvement in seconds,
a concept called Sequencing in the magic school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, yes, think as you please.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Yes, yes, think as you please.
Shirone thought it was more important to gather more information than to focus on Sequencing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You said there's a way to save our friends,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: right? What is it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You can tell us now, can't you?
Peope propped her chin and was lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: To be honest,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: it was a plan without guaranteed success.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But it was much more realistic than charging into the Angels' Haven unprepared.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Rather than a method,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: there is someone who loves humans very much.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I thought maybe they could help you guys.

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: Walking into the cluster of light,

blocks.speaker: Controller
blocks.content: Shirone couldn't contain his curiosity and asked again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really? Who is it?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Archangel Ikael-nim,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: the chief of the angels.
A pillar of light ascended.
Eternal and Immortal Ra (1)
After parting ways with the caretaker at Shamain's exit and walking for about 30 minutes,
they arrived at the wall where the tall tower of the Seventh Heaven, Araboth, was visible.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Peope said the women would be in the Sixth Heaven, Zebul.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How do we get into Zebul?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We don't have a way to pass the gate.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: That's right.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: So, we will go into Araboth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can I enter the place where gods live when we can't even enter Zebul?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: That's why we need the help of Archangel Ikael-nim.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Since she once held the position of Archangel,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: if we plead with her,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: she might let us in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That sounds even more strange.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can an archangel of the Sixth Heaven open the gate to the Seventh Heaven?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: That's because Archangel Ikael-nim is currently residing in the Seventh Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is there some reason we don't know about?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Archangel Ikael-nim committed a sin and was stripped of her title as Archangel.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: However,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: because Ra loved her so much,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: instead of casting her down as a fallen angel,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: he exiled her to Araboth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was quite a shocking story.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: From what Shirone had experienced,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: gods were stern judges who prioritized the law.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was strange that such a god would make an exception,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: and even more so that Ikael was exiled to Araboth instead of the Second Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What kind of angel is she?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What sin did she commit?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I don't know that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? How can you not know?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If she rose to the rank of Archangel,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: it must have been a huge issue.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: It probably was.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But we all forgot about it.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: We only know that Archangel Ikael-nim committed a great sin and is now exiled in Araboth.
Shirone felt a chill from Peope's words.
Saying they had forgotten meant they had once known but had their memories erased.
Even if someone's memory is erased,
someone else usually passes on that information.
Considering the speed at which information spreads,
it would be impossible unless the memories of all individuals were erased simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: First lifespans, now memories?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That's truly omnipotence.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: How old is this god anyway?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Maybe a hundred million years old?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Age is meaningless to a god.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: They are the beginning of everything.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: We don't know the origin,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: so we don't know the age.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It doesn't matter.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We're not going to fight the god.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We'll save the girls and come back right away.
Shirone listened carefully to what Canis said were girls,
not Arin.
Though they usually bicker,
it seemed he had unknowingly grown fond of them.
When they arrived at Araboth,
the group looked up at the wall.
Though it was the inner wall of Heaven,
it was taller than the outer wall.
Moreover,
there was no device in sight to summon Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Surely, we don't have to wait until she takes her dog for a walk,

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: right?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Hmph!

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: You only act tough now!

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Without Archangel Ikael-nim's help,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: you can't even enter Zebul.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I will apologize on his behalf.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He's just on edge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But what will we do now?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Alright!

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I'll try to initiate a conversation with Archangel Ikael-nim.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can you do that?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: No,

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: ...Are you joking right now?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: What can we do if that's the case?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But it's not entirely impossible.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Archangel Ikael-nim might be watching us.
